source.title: Understanding Trinity Terminology: Person and Nature
source.summary: In Christian theology, the Trinity is defined as one God in three persons. 'Nature' refers to what something is (essence/being), while 'person' refers to who someone is (distinct identity with will, knowledge, and relationality). God has one divine nature/essence but exists eternally as three distinct persons: Father, Son, and Holy Spirit. Each person is fully God, not one-third of God. This differs from tritheism (three gods) and modalism (one person appearing in three modes).
